perturbation.original_text: Consultant hereby irrevocably assigns to Company all right, title and interest in and to any information (including, without limitation, business plans and/or business information), technology, know-how, materials, notes, records, designs, ideas, inventions, improvements, devices, developments, discoveries, compositions, trade secrets, processes, methods and/or techniques, whether or not patentable or copyrightable, that are conceived, reduced to practice or made by Consultant alone or jointly with others in the course of performing the Services or through the use of Confidential Information (collectively, 111nventions"). Consultant agrees to sign, execute and acknowledge or cause to be signed, executed and acknowledged without cost, but at the expense of Company, any and all documents and to perform such acts as my be necessary, useful or convenient for the purposes of perfecting the foregoing assignments and obtaining, enforcing and defending intellectual property rights in any and all countries with respect to Inventions.
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant hereby irrevocably assigns to Company all right, title and interest in and to any information (including, without limitation, business plans and/or business information), technology, know-how, materials, notes, records, designs, ideas, improvements, devices, developments, discoveries, compositions, trade secrets, processes, methods and/or techniques, whether or not patentable or copyrightable, that are conceived, reduced to practice or made by Consultant alone or jointly with others in the course of performing the Services or through the use of Confidential Information (collectively, 111nventions"). Consultant agrees to sign, execute and acknowledge or cause to be signed, executed and acknowledged without cost, but at the expense of Company, any and all documents and to perform such acts as my be necessary, useful or convenient for the purposes of perfecting the foregoing assignments and obtaining, enforcing and defending intellectual property rights in any and all countries with respect to the Inventions.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "inventions" from the list of items assigned to the Company, but the changed text still refers to 'Inventions' in the subsequent sentence, creating an internal contradiction and creating uncertainty regarding what exactly the Consultant is assigning to the Company, since the original definition explicitly included 'inventions' but now the definition is being changed so that Inventions are no longer referenced and that creates an in-text contradiction, when mentioning the intellectual property rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consultant agrees to sign, execute and acknowledge or cause to be signed, executed and acknowledged without cost, but at the expense of Company, any and all documents and to perform such acts as my be necessary, useful or convenient for the purposes of perfecting the foregoing assignments and obtaining, enforcing and defending intellectual property rights in any and all countries with respect to Inventions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'inventions' from the defined list of items that the consultant assigns to the company. However, the subsequent sentence in both the changed text and the contradicted text still references 'Inventions' in the context of intellectual property rights. This creates a direct contradiction, as the defined scope of assigned items no longer explicitly includes 'inventions,' yet the text proceeds as if it does. This leads to uncertainty and potential unenforceability regarding the company's rights over inventions made by the consultant.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will commence on the Effective Date and will continue until termination as provided below. Either Consultant or Company may terminate this Agreement upon prior written notice thereof to the other party. Upon termination of this Agreement, all rights and duties of the parties hereunder shall cease except: Company shall be obliged to pay, within thirty (30) days after receipt of Consultant's final statement, all amounts owing to Consultant for unpaid Services completed by Consultant and related expenses, if any, in accordance with the provisions of Section 1 hereof, and Sections 2, 3, S(c), 6, 7, 8 and 10 shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will commence on the Effective Date and will continue until termination as provided below. Either Consultant or Company may terminate this Agreement upon prior written notice thereof to the other party. Upon termination of this Agreement, all rights and duties of the parties hereunder shall cease except: Company shall be obliged to pay, within thirty (30) days after receipt of Consultant's final statement, all amounts owing to Consultant for unpaid Services completed by Consultant and related expenses, if any, in accordance with the provisions of Section 1 hereof, and Sections 2, 3, 6, 7, 8 and 10 shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing Section 5 (c) from the list of sections that survive termination, while Section 5(c) originally addressed specific post-termination obligations, that are no longer mentioned. The other sections referenced here still refer back to it, but it doesn't exist.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, all rights and duties of the parties hereunder shall cease except: Company shall be obliged to pay, within thirty (30) days after receipt of Consultant's final statement, all amounts owing to Consultant for unpaid Services completed by Consultant and related expenses, if any, in accordance with the provisions of Section 1 hereof, and Sections 2, 3, 6, 7, 8 and 10 shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits Section 5(c) from the list of surviving clauses upon termination, whereas the original text included it. This creates a contradiction because other sections may rely on the obligations outlined in 5(c). By removing it and failing to address the obligations that should still be there, there's a direct conflict within the contract because obligations are expected to survive termination per the contradicted text."Upon termination of this Agreement, all rights and duties of the parties hereunder shall cease except... Sections 2, 3, 6, 7, 8 and 10 shall survive termination of this Agreement.", leading to uncertainty about the parties' responsibilities. This fits the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement (together with the Exhibits hereto) is the sole agreement and understanding between Company and Consultant concerning the subject matter hereof, and it supersedes any and all prior agreements and understandings with respect to such matter, whether written or oral, provided, that, except as set forth in Exhibit B. Any required notice shall be given in writing by customary means with receipt confirmed at the address of each party set forth below, or to such other address as either party may substitute by written notice to the other.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement (together with the Exhibits hereto) is the sole agreement and understanding between Company and Consultant concerning the subject matter hereof, and it supersedes any and all prior agreements and understandings with respect to such matter, whether written or oral. Any required notice shall be given in writing by customary means with receipt confirmed at the address of each party set forth below, or to such other address as either party may substitute by written notice to the other.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "provided, that, except as set forth in Exhibit B", the text now omits the exception that Exhibit B might contain additional or superseding agreements. This creates a direct contradiction if Exhibit B does exist and outlines additional terms because the main agreement states it's the *sole* agreement. If another section implicitly refers to Exhibit B governing certain situations but this exception is removed, the document becomes contradictory. Now the contract states that this agreement is the SOLE agreement, when exhibit B could say something contradictory. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement (together with the Exhibits hereto) is the sole agreement and understanding between Company and Consultant concerning the subject matter hereof, and it supersedes any and all prior agreements and understandings with respect to such matter, whether written or oral. Any required notice shall be given in writing by customary means with receipt confirmed at the address of each party set forth below, or to such other address as either party may substitute by written notice to the other.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified an exception for Exhibit B, acknowledging it might contain terms that supersede the main agreement. The changed text removes this exception, now stating that the agreement, along with exhibits, is the *sole* agreement. If Exhibit B outlines separate or additional terms, this change directly contradicts the document's original intention and Exhibit B's potential content, as the contradicted text is the same as the changed text but now asserts this is the SOLE agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction.
